\documentclass{article}

\usepackage{amsthm}

\newtheorem{theorem}{Theorem}

\begin{document}

\title{A Small Note on a Nonnegative Sum Bound}
\author{Test Author}
\maketitle

\begin{abstract}
We record a self-contained toy bound on a nonnegative finite sum. The result
is elementary and serves only as clean test material.
\end{abstract}

\section{A Bound on a Nonnegative Sum}
Let $a_1, \dots, a_n$ be real numbers with $a_i \ge 0$ for each $i$. We bound
their sum by a multiple of the largest term.

\begin{theorem}
\label{thm:sumbound}
Let $a_1, \dots, a_n \ge 0$ and let $M = \max_i a_i$. Then
$\sum_{i=1}^{n} a_i \le n M$.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
Each term satisfies $a_i \le M$ by the definition of $M$. Summing these $n$
inequalities gives $\sum_{i=1}^{n} a_i \le \sum_{i=1}^{n} M = n M$, as wanted.
\end{proof}

Theorem~\ref{thm:sumbound} is a discrete companion to the bound in
\cite{knuth1997}.

\end{document}
